task: I’m dealing with a guest complaint at work and want to handle it calmly without saying the wrong thing. Can you help me figure out what to write down and how to word it so I’m covered if it gets argued later?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — I can help with that.

Best approach: **write only the facts**, not opinions or guesses. Keep it simple, calm, and time-stamped so it’s clear what happened if it gets questioned later.

### What to write down
Include:
- **Room number / location**
- **Date and time**
- **What the guest said**
- **What you observed yourself**
- **What action you took**
- **Who you reported it to**
- **Whether the guest was informed**
- **Any follow-up needed**

### Good wording style
Use plain, neutral language like:
- “Guest reported…”
- “Observed…”
- “Maintenance was notified…”
- “Front desk was informed…”
- “Area was checked and…”
- “Guest advised…”

Avoid wording like:
- “Guest was being difficult”
- “They were lying”
- “It was no big deal”
- “They definitely caused it”

### Simple note template
You can write it like this:

**Room #:**  
**Date/Time:**  
**Issue reported:**  
**What was observed:**  
**Action taken:**  
**Who was notified:**  
**Guest informed?:**  
**Follow-up needed:**

### Example
**Room 214**  
**Date/Time:** 5/1/26 at 9:30 AM  
**Issue reported:** Guest reported water leaking under the bathroom sink.  
**What was observed:** Water was visible under the cabinet and the bathroom floor was wet.  
**Action taken:** Towels were placed down and the area was mopped. Maintenance was notified.  
**Who was notified:** Front desk and maintenance.  
**Guest informed?:** Yes, guest was advised to use the lobby restroom until the issue is fixed.  
**Follow-up needed:** Next shift to check whether maintenance completed the repair and confirm the floor stays dry.

If you want, I can help you write the **exact note** for your situation in a plain work style.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right idea. But I’d want it a little more careful than that if somebody might argue later.

I’d want:
- exact time if you know it
- who was there
- what the guest actually said, in quotes if you can
- what you saw yourself vs. what somebody told you
- what you did first
- who you notified and when
- anything damaged, wet, missing, or disturbed
- if photos were taken, note that too

Don’t write extra stuff like feelings or why you think it happened. Just facts.

A better way to word it is more like:

**At 9:15 a.m., guest in Room __ reported __.**
**I observed __.**
**I notified __ at __.**
**I placed / moved / checked __.**
**Guest was informed __.**
**Photos were taken / not taken.**

If you want, I can help you write the actual note for the complaint you’ve got right now. Just give me the room number and what happened plain.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — I can help you write it in a careful, factual way.

Send me:
- room number
- exact time, if known
- who was present
- what the guest said, if you remember the wording
- what you personally saw
- what you did first
- who you notified and when
- anything damaged, wet, missing, or disturbed
- whether photos were taken
- whether the guest was informed

I’ll turn it into a plain note that sticks to facts only and avoids wording that could be argued later.

user_simulator.text: Room 214. Around 9:15 a.m. Guest said, "There was water coming in under the bathroom sink and my stuff got wet." I was already in the hallway and saw water on the bathroom floor by the cabinet, but I didn’t see where it started from. Guest was there when I went in. I put towels down first so it wouldn’t spread, then I called the front desk and maintenance. Front desk was told at 9:18 a.m., maintenance at 9:19 a.m. The guest’s toiletry bag was sitting on the floor and the bottom of it was damp. I didn’t move any luggage. I took two photos on my phone before mopping. Guest was told maintenance was on the way and to keep the area clear. That’s about all I know for sure.

turns.agent_response:
Here’s a careful, factual note you can use:

**Room 214**  
**Date/Time:** Around 9:15 a.m.  
**Who was present:** Guest was present when I entered the room.  

**Guest statement:** Guest said, “There was water coming in under the bathroom sink and my stuff got wet.”  

**What I observed:** I saw water on the bathroom floor by the cabinet. I did not see where the water started from. The guest’s toiletry bag was on the floor and the bottom of it was damp.  

**Action taken:** I placed towels down first to keep the water from spreading, then I called the front desk and maintenance. I took two photos on my phone before mopping. I did not move any luggage.  

**Notifications:** Front desk notified at 9:18 a.m. Maintenance notified at 9:19 a.m.  

**Guest informed:** Guest was told maintenance was on the way and to keep the area clear.  

If you want, I can also make this into a **shorter front desk log version** or a **more formal incident report version**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. Clean and factual. I’d use it.

If I were writing it for the log, I’d probably keep it like this:

**Room 214**
**Around 9:15 a.m.** Guest was present when I entered the room.
**Guest statement:** Guest said, “There was water coming in under the bathroom sink and my stuff got wet.”
**Observed:** Water was on the bathroom floor by the cabinet. I did not see where it started. Guest’s toiletry bag was on the floor and the bottom was damp.
**Action taken:** I placed towels down first to keep the water from spreading, then notified the front desk and maintenance. I took two photos on my phone before mopping. I did not move any luggage.
**Notified:** Front desk at 9:18 a.m.; maintenance at 9:19 a.m.
**Guest informed:** Guest was told maintenance was on the way and to keep the area clear.

That’s about as tight as it needs to be. No extra talk, no guessing.
